steps.title: Recognize the appositive phrase
steps.content: The opening phrase “A recipient of the Nobel Prize in Chemistry and the Albert Einstein World Award of Science,” is an appositive—a noun phrase that renames another noun—set off by a comma before the main clause.

steps.title: Apply the appositive rule
steps.content:
An appositive must be immediately followed by the noun it describes. Choice B positions “chemist Ahmed Zewail” immediately after the appositive, correctly making him the noun it describes and the subject of the main clause.

Choices A, C, and D violate this rule by placing non-personal nouns (“the limits,” “the introduction,” “the result”) as the subject after an appositive describing a person.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
